mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 3.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rates signal a disinflationary environment with tightening monetary policy, which typically causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in disinflationary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) during a monetary tightening cycle indicates weakening demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or industrial production growth remains positive during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances with GDP growth > 2% annualized, or GDP growth remains positive without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through enhanced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances with INDPRO growth > 1% monthly, or industrial production declines without corresponding equity losses.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in high-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates labor market weakness, which signals reduced consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or payroll declines persist without corresponding equity losses.
